title: mentzelia pumila var. rusbyi
intro: Mentzelia pumila var. rusbyi, also known as Rusby's stickleaf, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Loasaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mentzelia pumila var. rusbyi include the relief of sore throats, coughs, and colds, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin irritations. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or dried and powdered for use in topical applications, though further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
